n. 快速奔跑；急促的跑步声；阵雨；骤雪
vt. 使紧追；使疾行：
vi. 疾走,急赶(away,off):；迅速旋转, 乱转：

scurries.t: v. 急匆匆地走( scurry的第三人称单数 )
scurries.d:
n rushing about hastily in an undignified way
v to move about or proceed hurriedly

scurrile.d:
a. Such as befits a buffoon or vulgar jester; grossly
   opprobrious or loudly jocose in language; scurrilous; as, scurrile
   taunts.

scurrilities.t: 粗俗下流( scurrility的名词复数 )；辱骂的言语

scurrility.t:
n. 谈吐卑劣粗鄙, 刻薄嘴, 下流
[法] 粗鲁的语言, 庸俗下流, 滥骂

scurrilous.t:
a. 说话粗鄙恶劣的, 下流的
[法] 语言粗鄙的, 庸俗的

scurrilously.t: adv. 粗俗地; 下流地, 粗野无礼地

scurry.t:
vi. 急赶, 急跑, 急转
n. 急赶, 急跑, 急转, 阵雪, 阵雨
scurry.d: v. to move about or proceed hurriedly

scurrying.t: v. 急匆匆地走( scurry的现在分词 )
scurrying.d:
v to move about or proceed hurriedly
s moving with great haste

scurryings.t: (scurrying 的复数) v. 急匆匆地走( scurry的现在分词 )
scurryings.d:
plural of scurrying
v to move about or proceed hurriedly
s moving with great haste

scurvily.t: adv. 粗鄙地, 下流地, 无礼地
scurvily.d: r in a despicable, ignoble manner

scurviness.t: n. 粗鄙, 恶劣, 无礼
scurviness.d:
n. The quality or state of being scurvy; vileness;
   meanness.

scurvy.t:
a. 下流的, 卑鄙的, 无礼的
n. 坏血病
scurvy.d: n. a condition caused by deficiency of ascorbic acid (vitamin C)

scut.t: n. 短尾, 可鄙的家伙

scuta.d:
n. pl. See Scutum.
pl.  of Scutum

scutage.d:
n. Shield money; commutation of service for a sum of money.
   See Escuage.

scutal.d: a. Of or pertaining to a shield.

scutate.t:
a. 有盾状的, 有鳞甲的, 圆盾形的
[医] 鳞的, 鼓室盾板的
scutate.d:
a. Buckler-shaped; round or nearly round.
a. Protected or covered by bony or horny plates, or large
   scales.

scutch.t:
n. 打麻[棉]机
v. 将麻,棉等的纤维打散, 以打麻机打
scutch.d:
v. t. To beat or whip; to drub.
v. t. To separate the woody fiber from (flax, hemp, etc.) by
   beating; to swingle.
v. t. To loosen and dress the fiber of (cotton or silk) by
   beating; to free (fibrous substances) from dust by beating and blowing.
n. A wooden instrument used in scutching flax and hemp.
n. The woody fiber of flax; the refuse of scutched flax.

scutched.t:
vt. 以打麻机将棉麻等的纤维打散
n. 打麻机；打棉机

scutcheon.d:
n a flat protective covering (on a door or wall etc) to prevent soiling by dirty fingers
n a shield; especially one displaying a coat of arms

scutcheoned.d: a. Emblazoned on or as a shield.

scutcher.d:
n. One who scutches.
n. An implement or machine for scutching hemp, flax, or
   cotton; etc.; a scutch; a scutching machine.

scutching.t: 梳打, 清棉, 打麻, 开(布)幅
scutching.d: p. pr. & vb. n. of Scutch

scute.t:
n. 鳞甲, 盾板
[医] 鳞, 鼓室盾板
scute.d: n. large bony or horny plate as on an armadillo or turtle or the underside of a snake

scutella.d:
n. pl. See Scutellum.
n. See Scutellum, n., 2.
pl.  of Scutellum

scutellar.t: a. 盾片的; 角质鳞片的

scutellaria.d: n an asterid dicot genus that includes the skullcaps

scutellate.t: a. 有小盾板的, 盾形的

scutellated.d:
a. Formed like a plate or salver; composed of platelike
   surfaces; as, the scutellated bone of a sturgeon.
a. Having the tarsi covered with broad transverse scales,
   or scutella; -- said of certain birds.

scutellation.t: [动]鳞片排列, 刻纹[直片]排列
scutellation.d:
n. the entire covering, or mode of arrangement, of
   scales, as on the legs and feet of a bird.

scutelliform.d:
a. Scutellate.
a. Having the form of a scutellum.

scutellum.t:
n. 小盾板, 角质鳞片
[医] 小盾片(昆虫)
scutellum.d:
n. A rounded apothecium having an elevated rim formed of
   the proper thallus, the fructification of certain lichens.
n. The third of the four pieces forming the upper part of a
   thoracic segment of an insect. It follows the scutum, and is followed
   by the small postscutellum; a scutella. See Thorax.
n. One of the transverse scales on the tarsi and toes of
   birds; a scutella.

scutes.t:
(scute 的复数) n. 鳞甲, 盾板
[医] 鳞, 鼓室盾板
scutes.d:
plural of scute
n. large bony or horny plate as on an armadillo or turtle or the underside of a snake

scutibranch.d:
a. Scutibranchiate.
n. One of the Scutibranchiata.

scutiger.d:
n. Any species of chilopod myriapods of the genus Scutigera.
   They sometimes enter buildings and prey upon insects.

scutigerella.t: [网络] 么蚰；多足类的蚰蜒

scuts.t: (scut 的复数) n. 短尾, 可鄙的家伙
scuts.d:
plural of scut
n. a short erect tail

scuttered.t: (scutter 的过去时) vi. 急跑；疾走

scuttering.t: (scutter 的现在分词) vi. 急跑；疾走

scutters.t: (scutter 的复数) vi. 急跑；疾走

scuttle.t:
n. 箩, 筐, 急速逃走, 天窗, 小舱口
vi. 急速逃走, 急忙撤退
vt. 凿沉, 毁坏
scuttle.d: n. container for coal; shaped to permit pouring the coal onto the fire

scuttlebutt.t: n. 饮水容器, 清水桶, 谣言
scuttlebutt.d: n a report (often malicious) about the behavior of other people

scuttled.t: v. 使船沉没( scuttle的过去式和过去分词 ); 快跑, 急走

scuttler.t: n. 疾驰者; (尤指短跑的)赛跑运动员; 逃避者; (尤指为获取保险金而)沉船者

scuttlers.t: (scuttler 的复数) n. 疾驰者; (尤指短跑的)赛跑运动员; 逃避者; (尤指为获取保险金而)沉船者

scuttles.t:
n. 天窗( scuttle的名词复数 )
v. 使船沉没( scuttle的第三人称单数 ); 快跑, 急走
scuttles.d:
n container for coal; shaped to permit pouring the coal onto the fire
n an entrance equipped with a hatch; especially a passageway between decks of a ship
v to move about or proceed hurriedly

scuttling.d: v to move about or proceed hurriedly

scutum.t:
n. 长形盾, 鳞甲
[医] 鼓室盾板, 甲状软骨, 髌骨, 盾片(昆虫)
scutum.d:
n. An oblong shield made of boards or wickerwork covered with
   leather, with sometimes an iron rim; -- carried chiefly by the
   heavy-armed infantry.
n. A penthouse or awning.
n. The second and largest of the four parts forming the upper
   surface of a thoracic segment of an insect. It is preceded by the
   prescutum and followed by the scutellum. See the Illust. under Thorax.
n. One of the two lower valves of the operculum of a barnacle.

scutums.t:
(scutum 的复数) n. 长形盾, 鳞甲
[医] 鼓室盾板, 甲状软骨, 髌骨, 盾片(昆虫)
scutums.d:
plural of scutum
n. An oblong shield made of boards or wickerwork covered with
   leather, with sometimes an iron rim; -- carried chiefly by the
   heavy-armed infantry.
n. A penthouse or awning.
n. The second and largest of the four parts forming the upper
   surface of a thoracic segment of an insect. It is preceded by the
   prescutum and followed by the scutellum. See the Illust. under Thorax.
n. One of the two lower valves of the operculum of a barnacle.

scuzz.t: n. 邋遢的人, 脏东西

scuzzies.t: (scuzzy 的复数) adj.<美口>肮脏的,邋遢的,阴暗的

scuzziest.t: (scuzzy 的最高级) adj.<美口>肮脏的,邋遢的,阴暗的

scuzzy.t: a. <美口>肮脏的,邋遢的,阴暗的

scv.t: abbr. single concave 单式的凹面

scybala.d: n. pl. Hardened masses of feces.

scye.d:
n. Arm scye, a cutter's term for the armhole or part of the
   armhole of the waist of a garnment.

scyliorhinidae.t: [网络] 猫鲨科；猫鲛科；二名法

scylla.t: n. 锡拉岩礁（靠近意大利一边的危险岩石）
scylla.d: n. (Greek mythology) a sea nymph transformed into a sea monster who lived on one side of a narrow strait; drowned and devoured sailors who tried to escape Charybdis (a whirlpool) on the other side of the strait

scyllarian.d:
n. One of a family (Scyllaridae) of macruran Crustacea,
   remarkable for the depressed form of the body, and the broad, flat
   antennae. Also used adjectively.

scyllite.d:
n. A white crystalline substance of a sweetish taste,
   resembling inosite and metameric with dextrose. It is extracted from
   the kidney of the dogfish (of the genus Scylium), the shark, and the
   skate.

scyllitol.t: 鲨[异]肌醇, 青蟹肌醇

scymnol.t:
[化] 鲨胆甾醇
[医] 鲨胆甾醇, 鲨胆固醇

scyphi.d: n an ancient Greek drinking cup; two handles and footed base

scyphistoma.t: n. [动]螅状幼体,钵口幼虫(亦作scyphistome)
scyphistoma.d:
n. The young attached larva of Discophora in the stage
   when it resembles a hydroid, or actinian.

scyphozoa.d:
n coelenterates in which the polyp stage is absent or at least inconspicuous: jellyfishes
n any of various usually free-swimming marine coelenterates having a gelatinous medusoid stage as the dominant phase of its life cycle

scyphozoan.d: n any of various usually free-swimming marine coelenterates having a gelatinous medusoid stage as the dominant phase of its life cycle

scyphozoans.d:
plural of scyphozoan
n any of various usually free-swimming marine coelenterates having a gelatinous medusoid stage as the dominant phase of its life cycle

scyphus.d: n an ancient Greek drinking cup; two handles and footed base

scyra.t: [法] 郡, 郡民, 郡的居民

scythe.d:

v. cut with a scythe

scythed.d: a. Armed scythes, as a chariot.

scythes.t:

n an edge tool for cutting grass; has a long handle that must be held with both hands and a curved blade that moves parallel to the ground
v cut with a scythe

scythestone.d: n. A stone for sharpening scythes; a whetstone.

scythia.d: n an ancient area of Eurasia extending from the Black Sea to the Aral Sea that was populated by Scythians from the eighth to the fourth century BC

a. of or relating to the ancient Scythians or their culture or language

scythians.t:
(Scythian 的复数) n.斯基台人[语]

scythians.d:

n. the Iranian language spoken by the ancient Scythians
a. of or relating to the ancient Scythians or their culture or language

scything.t: v. （长柄）大镰刀( scythe的现在分词 )
